A sample-Al interlayer-Al monitor sandwich was scotch-
fastened to a 50*50 mm, 0.1 mm thick Al plate placed
normally to proton beam. The experimental samples and
the monitors were manufactured to within the accuracy
that provided for their identical geometric dimensions.

disregarding the error in the monitoring cross section.

including the error in the monitoring cross section.

and gamma-spectrum transients and allowing for the dead
time and count loss of the spectrometer.

heading  "ISOMER" contains space only, production cross
section includes isomer state production too.

obtained from experimental data via simple
The error in the presented cross sections for
production of residuals consists of the errors in their
production rates and the errors in determining the
proton flux densities. The major uncertainties of
reaction rates are represented by statistical error,
by absolute spectrometer effectiveness and by
uncertainty in absolute abundance, but the absolute
uncertainties of the monitoring reaction rates.
The uncertainty in proton flux density is determined
solely by the monitoring reaction cross section
total absorption peak with due allowance for correction
of unresolved gamma-lines, background under the peak
and gamma-spectrum transients and allowing for the dead
time and count loss of the spectrometer.
